2605.03570.title: Disentangling Shared and Task-Specific Representations from Multi-Modal Clinical Data
2605.03570.paper_summary: Real-world clinical data is inherently multimodal, providing complementary evidence that mirrors the practical necessity of jointly assessing multiple related outcomes. Although multi-task learning can improve efficiency by sharing information across outcomes, existing approaches often fail to balance shared representation learning with outcome-specific modeling. Hard parameter sharing can trigger negative transfer when task gradients conflict, while flexible sharing may still entangle shared and task-specific signals. To address this, we propose a multi-task framework built on a unified Transformer for multimodal fusion, augmented with Orthogonal Task Decomposition (OrthTD) to split patient representations into shared and task-specific subspaces and impose a geometric orthogonality constraint to reduce redundancy and isolate task-specific signals. We evaluated OrthTD on a real-world cohort of 12,430 surgical patients for predicting four outcomes. OrthTD achieved average AUC (area under the receiver operating characteristic curve) of 87.5% and average AUPRC (area under the precision-recall curve) of 37.2%, consistently outperformed advanced tabular and multi-task methods. Notably, OrthTD achieves substantial gains in AUPRC, indicating superior performance in identifying rare events within imbalanced clinical data. These results suggest that enforcing non-redundant shared and task-specific representations can improve multi-outcome prediction from multimodal clinical data.
2605.03570.paper_summary_zh: 真實世界的臨床數據本質上是多模態的，提供了互補的證據，反映了共同評估多個相關結果的實際需求。儘管多任務學習可以通過在結果之間共享信息來提高效率，但現有的方法往往未能平衡共享表示學習與結果特定建模。當任務梯度衝突時，硬參數共享可能會引發負轉移，而靈活共享仍可能會糾纏共享和任務特定信號。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個基於統一Transformer的多任務框架，用於多模態融合，並增強了正交任務分解（OrthTD），以將患者表示分割為共享和任務特定的子空間，並施加幾何正交約束以減少冗餘並隔離任務特定信號。我們在一個包含12,430名外科患者的真實世界隊列中評估了OrthTD，以預測四個結果。OrthTD實現了87.5%的平均AUC（接收者操作特徵曲線下面積）和37.2%的平均AUPRC（精確率-召回曲線下面積），持續超越先進的表格和多任務方法。值得注意的是，OrthTD在AUPRC上取得了顯著增益，表明在識別不平衡臨床數據中的稀有事件方面表現優越。這些結果表明，強制執行非冗餘的共享和任務特定表示可以改善來自多模態臨床數據的多結果預測。
2605.03570.author: He Lyu et.al.
2605.03570.authors: He Lyu, Huolin Zeng, Junren Wang, Huazhen Yang, Linchao He, Yong Chen, Zhirui Li, Andreas Maier, Siming Bayer, Huan Song
